== Notes for [[Item:Q2200|Q2200]] ==

RESEARCH NOTES:
<p>King of Kashmir and Gandhara</p>
<p>VICEROY OF TAXILA; POSSIBLY SAME AS "JALAUKA", KING OF KASHMIR AND GANDHARA
<p>(WHO WAS FATHER OF SAMPRATI, RULER OF THE UNITED MAURYA EMPIRE, c. 225/4 BC- c,</p>
<p>216/5 BC)</p>
<p>SOURCE NOTES:</p>
<p>http://www.american-pictures.com/genealogy/persons/per04842.htm#0</p>
<p>{geni:about_me} [https://books.google.com/books?id=Tn5oVOER2ogC&pg=PA51&lpg=PA51&dq=Kunala,+King+of+Kashmir+%26+Gandhara&source=bl&ots=MHNXOY4kkZ&sig=jcWmqWneXFMDhNrMVW6v6ADsD3c&hl=en&sa=X&ei=n8HWVI6kIoWigwTLwIDQCQ&ved=0CCoQ6AEwAjgK#v=onepage&q=Kunala%2C%20King%20of%20Kashmir%20%26%20Gandhara&f=false '''Kinnaur in the Himalayas]: Mythology to Modernity, By Shiva Chandra Bajpai
<p>Lord Garbhodakasayi (Sahasra-sira Purusa) VISHNU, from whose navel sprang a lotus, from which came Lord Brahmā</p>
<p>Lord Brahmā</p>
<p>Marici (generated from the mind of Lord Brahmā) = Kala</p>
<p>Kasyapa = Aditi, daughter of Prajapati Daksha (son of Lord Brahmā) & Prasuti</p>
<p>Vaivasvata Manu</p>
<p>Mahārāja Ikshvaku, 1st King of Kosala</p>
<p>Kikshi</p>
<p>Vikukshi</p>
<p>Baana</p>
<p>Anaranya</p>
<p>Pruthu</p>
<p>Trishanku</p>
<p>Dhundumaara</p>
<p>Yuvanaashva</p>
<p>Mandhaata</p>
<p>Susandhi</p>
<p>Dhruvasandhi</p>
<p>Bharata</p>
<p>Asita</p>
<p>Sagara, whose mother was poisoned</p>
<p>Asamanja</p>
<p>Amshuman</p>
<p>Diliipa</p>
<p>Bhageeratha</p>
<p>Kakutstha</p>
<p>Raghu</p>
<p>Pravriddha (Kalmashapaada)</p>
<p>Shankana</p>
<p>Sudarshana</p>
<p>Agnivarsna</p>
<p>Shiigraga</p>
<p>Maru</p>
<p>Prashushruka</p>
<p>Ambariisha</p>
<p>Nahusha, the emperor</p>
<p>Yayaati</p>
<p>Naabhaaga</p>
<p>Aja</p>
<p>Daśaratha</p>
<p>Lord Śrī Rāmacandra (Rāma) = Sita, daughter of Bhumidevi; adopted by King Janaka</p>
<p>Kusa (Kusha), King of Kosala</p>
<p>Atithia, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Nisadha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Nala, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Nabhas, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Pundarika, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Ksemadhrtvan, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Devanika, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Ahinaga, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Pariyatra, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Danu, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Nalavajranabha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Urnanabha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Srnkhala, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Vyusitasva, King of Kosala = Princess Bhadra, dau. King Kaksivant</p>
<p>Visvasaha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Hiranyanabha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Pusya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Dhruvasandhi, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Sudrasena, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Agnivarna, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Sighrasva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Maru, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Prasusruta, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Susandhi, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Amarsa, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Shasvant, King of Kosala = Vasundhara Paurava</p>
<p>Visrutavant, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Prince of Kosala</p>
<p>Brhatksaya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Uruksaya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Prince of Kosala, son of Uruksaya</p>
<p>Prativyoma, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Divakara, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Brhadasva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Bhanuratha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Pratitasva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Supratita, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Marudeva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Kinnarasva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Suparna, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Sumitra [I] Amitrajit, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Bharadvaja, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Dharma, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Krtanjaya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Rananjaya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Jaya-Sena, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Prince Haryanka</p>
<p>Bhattiya, King of Magadha (the usurper) = dau. King Vishvajati of Magadha</p>
<p>Bimbisara, King of Magadha (544-491 BCE) = Chellena Licchavi</p>
<p>Ajatasatru Kunika, King of Magadha = Princess Vajra of Kosala, dau. King Prasenajit</p>
<p>Nandivardha Anuruddha, King of Magadha = Bhadda, dau. King of Bengal</p>
<p>Nagadashoka, King of Magadha</p>
<p>Sisunaga, King of Magadha</p>
<p>Kalasoka Kakavara, King of Magadha</p>
<p>Ksemadharma, King of Magadha = dau. Gopaditya Gonandiya of Kashmir</p>
<p>Dhananada [Xandrames], King of Magadha = sister of Dhana Nanda of Magadha</p>
<p>Nandini = Chandragupta Maurya [Sandrakottes], Mahārāja of India (324-301 BCE)</p>
<p>Princess Subhadrangi Dharmi = Bindusar/Vindusara, Mahārāja of India (301-269 BCE)</p>
<p>Ashoka “the Great”, Devanampiya Piyadassi (304-232 BCE) = Tissarakkha Karuvaki</p>
<p>Kunala, King of Kashmir = dau. King Devapala of Nepal & a Tibetan princess</p>
<p>Brihadratha [II], Mahārāja of India (187-181 BCE) = Princess Berenice of Bactria</p>
<p>Princess Sundari of India = King Demetrius of Bactria (200-185 BCE)</p>
<p>Princess Alexandra of Bactria = King Agathocles of Bactria (165-160 BCE)</p>
<p>Princess Agathoclea of Bactria = Menander I (Soter), King of Bactria (155-150 BCE)</p>
<p>Strato I, King of Bactria = sister of Vasumitra, Mahārāja of India</p>
<p>Apollodotus II, King of Bactria</p>
<p>Hipposatos, King of Bactria</p>
<p>Telephus, King of Bactria</p>
<p>Princess Calliope of Bactria = King Hermaeus of Bactria</p>
<p>Princess of Bactria = Kuyula Kadphises [Kilu-dzilu-kiak], King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Vima Kadphises of the Kushans = granddaughter of Susaramana, Mahārāja of India</p>
<p>Kanishka I (Devaputra), King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Huvishka I, King of the Kushans (130-150 CE) = dau. of Nahapa, King of the Sakyae</p>
<p>Vasudeva I, King of the Kushans (150-160 CE)</p>
<p>Kanishka III, King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Vasudeva II [Po T’iao], King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Vasudeva III, King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Vasudeva, King of Kabul</p>
<p>Princess Ifra Hormiz of the Kushan = Hormizd II Sásání, Sháh of Persia</p>
<p>Shápúr II, Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Sithil-Horak of the Scyths & Parthians</p>
<p>Shápúr III, Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Tihe-He of Shan-Shan</p>
<p>Yazdegerd I Sásání, Sháh of Persia</p>
<p>Bahram V Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Sapinud, dau. Emperor Kumaragupta (415-455)</p>
<p>Yazdegerd II Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Dhanakha [Dinak] the Hephthalite</p>
<p>Peroz (Firuz) Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Mihrandukht, an Iberian princess</p>
<p>Kubad I Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Nevandukht the Hephthalite</p>
<p>Khusraw I (the Just) Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Kayen of the Turks</p>
<p>Hormizd IV Sásání, Sháh of Persia = daughter of Khurad, King of the Hephthalites</p>
<p>Khusraw II (Parvez) Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Sirin of Turkestan</p>
<p>Sharíyár Sásání, Sháh of Persia </p>
<p>Yazdegerd III, Shah of Persia = Maha Talat</p>
<p>Princess Shahrbanu of Persia, dau. Yazdegerd III (f2) = Husayn ibn ‘Alí Abu-Talib, 3rd Shiite Imám </p>
<p>‘Alí ibn Husayn, Zaynu’l-Ábidín (658-713), 4th Shiite Imám = Fatimah, dau. of Hasan (2nd Imám)</p>
<p>Muhammal ibn ‘Alí ibn Husayn, al-Báqir, 5th Shiite Imám (676-743) = Farwah, g-g-dau. of Abu Bakr, 1st Sunni Caliph</p>
<p>Ja‘far ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Alí, as-Sádiq, 6th Shiite Imám (708-765) = Fatima</p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Ja‘far ibn Muhammad (736-760) brother of ALI AL-URAIDI</p>
<p>Muhammad ibn Isma’il ibn Ja‘far </p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Ja‘far ibn Muhammad (721-755) = </p>
<p>Muhammad ibn Isma’il ibn Ja‘far = </p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Muhammad ibn Isma’il = </p>
<p>Ahmad ibn Isma’il ibn Muhammad = </p>
<p>Ubaydu’lláh ibn Ahmad, “al-Mahdi”, Fatimid Caliph of Egypt (846-934)</p>
<p>Muhammad ibn Ubaydu’lláh, “al-Qá’im”, Fatimid Caliph of Egypt (873-946) = </p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Muhammad, “al-Mansur”, Fatimid Caliph of Egypt (901-952) (d. 953) = </p>
<p>Princess Dihja bint al-Mansur (f3) = Qarays ibn ‘Abbad (b.c. 920)</p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Qarays, Judge of Seville and Imam</p>
<p>Kadi ‘Abu’l-Qasim Muhamad I ibn Isma’il, Chamberlain of Seville(1023-1042)</p>
<p>‘Abu ‘Amr ‘Abbad ibn Muhammad ll, Chamberlain of Seville </p>
<p>‘Abu’l-Qasim Muhammad al-Mu‘tamid, Emir of Seville = I‘tamid </p>
<p>Zaida (Isabella) (1075-1107) (f3) = King Alfonso VI de León y Castile </p>
<p>Sancha, (F4) Princess of Leon and Castile = Rodrigo Gonzales (“El Franco”)</p>
<p>Rodrigo Rodriguez de Lara (b. 1123) = Elvira Garcia de Azarga</p>
<p>Sancha Rodriguez de Lara (f5) = Gonsalo Ruis II Giron (d. 1234)</p>
<p>Aldonza Gonzales de Giron (f6) = Ramiro Fruelaz, Señor de Cifontes </p>
<p>Aldonza Ramirez Fruelaz (f7) = Arias Diaz de Valderabano de Asturias</p>
<p>Maria de Henestrosa (f8) = Fernan Gonzalez de Henestrosa</p>
<p>Juan Fernández de Henestrosa = Sancha Gonzalez Villegas </p>
<p>Lope Alvarez de Henestrosa = Elvira Arias de Cuadros </p>
<p>Teresa López de Córdoba (f9) = Gomez Suarez de Figueroa </p>
<p>Lorenzo Suárez de Figueroa López de Córdoba, Maestre de Santiago = Maria Catalina de Orozco Garcia</p>
<p>Catalina Suárez de Figueroa (f10) = Inigo Lopez de Mendoza (120) </p>
<p>Mencía de Mendoza y Figueroa (f11) = Pedro Fernandez de Velasco</p>
<p>Leonor de la Vega y Velasco (f12) = Juan Tellez Giron</p>
<p>Juan Téllez Girón, "El Santo" = Maria de la Cueva y Toledo </p>
<p>Magdalena Girón de la Cueva (f13) = Juan Manuel Manrique de Lara</p>
<p>Luisa Manrique de Lara, duquesa de Nájera (f14) = Bernardo de Cardenas</p>
<p>Ana María de Cárdenas y Manrique de Lara (f15) = Jorge de Lancaster lll</p>
<p>MARIA GUADALUPE DE LANCASTER (f16) = MANUEL PONCE DE LEON</p>
<p>JOAQUIN PONCE DE LEON = ANNA MARIA SPINOLA DE LA CERDA</p>
<p>ANTONIO PONCE DE LEON = MARIA LUISA DE BOURBON</p>
<p>PABLO PONCE DE LEON EXILED TO CUYO, PHILIPPINES = GERTRUDES de AGUILAR - PALAWAN (130)</p>
<p>VICENTE PONCE DE LEON = CONSUELO ARGUELLES</p>
<p>CONSOLACION PONCE DE LEON (f17) = GREGORIO AUSTRIA</p>
<p>PABLO PONCE DE LEON AUSTRIA = FRANCISCA MACMAC</p>
<p>MARCELA AUSTRIA = MARIANO ZAMBALES (f18)</p>
<p>ELEUTERIO AUSTRIA ZAMBALES = JACOBA CARRANZA </p>
<p>MATIA CARRANZA ZAMBALES (f19) = FAUSTINO USTARIS</p>
<p>AGUSTIN ZAMBALES USTARIS = CARIDAD MAGBANUA PAREDES</p>
<p>AZUCENA USTARES (f20) = SABAS ROBLES ACOSTA</p>
<p>DATU ANDREI USTARES ACOSTA = ELLEN LAUROS BARRAQUIAS</p>
<p>ZIELLZA BARRAQUIAS ACOSTA (f21)</p>
<p>RAJA ANDREI BARRAQUIAS ACOSTA (141)</p>
<p>-- MERGED NOTE ------------</p>
<p>{geni:about_me} [https://books.google.com/books?id=Tn5oVOER2ogC&pg=PA51&lpg=PA51&dq=Kunala,+King+of+Kashmir+%26+Gandhara&source=bl&ots=MHNXOY4kkZ&sig=jcWmqWneXFMDhNrMVW6v6ADsD3c&hl=en&sa=X&ei=n8HWVI6kIoWigwTLwIDQCQ&ved=0CCoQ6AEwAjgK#v=onepage&q=Kunala%2C%20King%20of%20Kashmir%20%26%20Gandhara&f=false '''Kinnaur in the Himalayas]: Mythology to Modernity, By Shiva Chandra Bajpai
<p>Lord Garbhodakasayi (Sahasra-sira Purusa) VISHNU, from whose navel sprang a lotus, from which came Lord Brahmā</p>
<p>Lord Brahmā</p>
<p>Marici (generated from the mind of Lord Brahmā) = Kala</p>
<p>Kasyapa = Aditi, daughter of Prajapati Daksha (son of Lord Brahmā) & Prasuti</p>
<p>Vaivasvata Manu</p>
<p>Mahārāja Ikshvaku, 1st King of Kosala</p>
<p>Kikshi</p>
<p>Vikukshi</p>
<p>Baana</p>
<p>Anaranya</p>
<p>Pruthu</p>
<p>Trishanku</p>
<p>Dhundumaara</p>
<p>Yuvanaashva</p>
<p>Mandhaata</p>
<p>Susandhi</p>
<p>Dhruvasandhi</p>
<p>Bharata</p>
<p>Asita</p>
<p>Sagara, whose mother was poisoned</p>
<p>Asamanja</p>
<p>Amshuman</p>
<p>Diliipa</p>
<p>Bhageeratha</p>
<p>Kakutstha</p>
<p>Raghu</p>
<p>Pravriddha (Kalmashapaada)</p>
<p>Shankana</p>
<p>Sudarshana</p>
<p>Agnivarsna</p>
<p>Shiigraga</p>
<p>Maru</p>
<p>Prashushruka</p>
<p>Ambariisha</p>
<p>Nahusha, the emperor</p>
<p>Yayaati</p>
<p>Naabhaaga</p>
<p>Aja</p>
<p>Daśaratha</p>
<p>Lord Śrī Rāmacandra (Rāma) = Sita, daughter of Bhumidevi; adopted by King Janaka</p>
<p>Kusa (Kusha), King of Kosala</p>
<p>Atithia, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Nisadha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Nala, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Nabhas, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Pundarika, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Ksemadhrtvan, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Devanika, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Ahinaga, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Pariyatra, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Danu, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Nalavajranabha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Urnanabha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Srnkhala, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Vyusitasva, King of Kosala = Princess Bhadra, dau. King Kaksivant</p>
<p>Visvasaha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Hiranyanabha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Pusya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Dhruvasandhi, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Sudrasena, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Agnivarna, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Sighrasva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Maru, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Prasusruta, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Susandhi, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Amarsa, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Shasvant, King of Kosala = Vasundhara Paurava</p>
<p>Visrutavant, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Prince of Kosala</p>
<p>Brhatksaya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Uruksaya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Prince of Kosala, son of Uruksaya</p>
<p>Prativyoma, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Divakara, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Brhadasva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Bhanuratha, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Pratitasva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Supratita, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Marudeva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Kinnarasva, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Suparna, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Sumitra [I] Amitrajit, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Bharadvaja, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Dharma, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Krtanjaya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Rananjaya, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Jaya-Sena, King of Kosala</p>
<p>Prince Haryanka</p>
<p>Bhattiya, King of Magadha (the usurper) = dau. King Vishvajati of Magadha</p>
<p>Bimbisara, King of Magadha (544-491 BCE) = Chellena Licchavi</p>
<p>Ajatasatru Kunika, King of Magadha = Princess Vajra of Kosala, dau. King Prasenajit</p>
<p>Nandivardha Anuruddha, King of Magadha = Bhadda, dau. King of Bengal</p>
<p>Nagadashoka, King of Magadha</p>
<p>Sisunaga, King of Magadha</p>
<p>Kalasoka Kakavara, King of Magadha</p>
<p>Ksemadharma, King of Magadha = dau. Gopaditya Gonandiya of Kashmir</p>
<p>Dhananada [Xandrames], King of Magadha = sister of Dhana Nanda of Magadha</p>
<p>Nandini = Chandragupta Maurya [Sandrakottes], Mahārāja of India (324-301 BCE)</p>
<p>Princess Subhadrangi Dharmi = Bindusar/Vindusara, Mahārāja of India (301-269 BCE)</p>
<p>Ashoka “the Great”, Devanampiya Piyadassi (304-232 BCE) = Tissarakkha Karuvaki</p>
<p>Kunala, King of Kashmir = dau. King Devapala of Nepal & a Tibetan princess</p>
<p>Brihadratha [II], Mahārāja of India (187-181 BCE) = Princess Berenice of Bactria</p>
<p>Princess Sundari of India = King Demetrius of Bactria (200-185 BCE)</p>
<p>Princess Alexandra of Bactria = King Agathocles of Bactria (165-160 BCE)</p>
<p>Princess Agathoclea of Bactria = Menander I (Soter), King of Bactria (155-150 BCE)</p>
<p>Strato I, King of Bactria = sister of Vasumitra, Mahārāja of India</p>
<p>Apollodotus II, King of Bactria</p>
<p>Hipposatos, King of Bactria</p>
<p>Telephus, King of Bactria</p>
<p>Princess Calliope of Bactria = King Hermaeus of Bactria</p>
<p>Princess of Bactria = Kuyula Kadphises [Kilu-dzilu-kiak], King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Vima Kadphises of the Kushans = granddaughter of Susaramana, Mahārāja of India</p>
<p>Kanishka I (Devaputra), King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Huvishka I, King of the Kushans (130-150 CE) = dau. of Nahapa, King of the Sakyae</p>
<p>Vasudeva I, King of the Kushans (150-160 CE)</p>
<p>Kanishka III, King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Vasudeva II [Po T’iao], King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Vasudeva III, King of the Kushans</p>
<p>Vasudeva, King of Kabul</p>
<p>Princess Ifra Hormiz of the Kushan = Hormizd II Sásání, Sháh of Persia</p>
<p>Shápúr II, Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Sithil-Horak of the Scyths & Parthians</p>
<p>Shápúr III, Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Tihe-He of Shan-Shan</p>
<p>Yazdegerd I Sásání, Sháh of Persia</p>
<p>Bahram V Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Sapinud, dau. Emperor Kumaragupta (415-455)</p>
<p>Yazdegerd II Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Dhanakha [Dinak] the Hephthalite</p>
<p>Peroz (Firuz) Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Mihrandukht, an Iberian princess</p>
<p>Kubad I Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Nevandukht the Hephthalite</p>
<p>Khusraw I (the Just) Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Kayen of the Turks</p>
<p>Hormizd IV Sásání, Sháh of Persia = daughter of Khurad, King of the Hephthalites</p>
<p>Khusraw II (Parvez) Sásání, Sháh of Persia = Princess Sirin of Turkestan</p>
<p>Sharíyár Sásání, Sháh of Persia </p>
<p>Yazdegerd III, Shah of Persia = Maha Talat</p>
<p>Princess Shahrbanu of Persia, dau. Yazdegerd III (f2) = Husayn ibn ‘Alí Abu-Talib, 3rd Shiite Imám </p>
<p>‘Alí ibn Husayn, Zaynu’l-Ábidín (658-713), 4th Shiite Imám = Fatimah, dau. of Hasan (2nd Imám)</p>
<p>Muhammal ibn ‘Alí ibn Husayn, al-Báqir, 5th Shiite Imám (676-743) = Farwah, g-g-dau. of Abu Bakr, 1st Sunni Caliph</p>
<p>Ja‘far ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Alí, as-Sádiq, 6th Shiite Imám (708-765) = Fatima</p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Ja‘far ibn Muhammad (736-760) brother of ALI AL-URAIDI</p>
<p>Muhammad ibn Isma’il ibn Ja‘far </p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Ja‘far ibn Muhammad (721-755) = </p>
<p>Muhammad ibn Isma’il ibn Ja‘far = </p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Muhammad ibn Isma’il = </p>
<p>Ahmad ibn Isma’il ibn Muhammad = </p>
<p>Ubaydu’lláh ibn Ahmad, “al-Mahdi”, Fatimid Caliph of Egypt (846-934)</p>
<p>Muhammad ibn Ubaydu’lláh, “al-Qá’im”, Fatimid Caliph of Egypt (873-946) = </p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Muhammad, “al-Mansur”, Fatimid Caliph of Egypt (901-952) (d. 953) = </p>
<p>Princess Dihja bint al-Mansur (f3) = Qarays ibn ‘Abbad (b.c. 920)</p>
<p>Isma’il ibn Qarays, Judge of Seville and Imam</p>
<p>Kadi ‘Abu’l-Qasim Muhamad I ibn Isma’il, Chamberlain of Seville(1023-1042)</p>
<p>‘Abu ‘Amr ‘Abbad ibn Muhammad ll, Chamberlain of Seville </p>
<p>‘Abu’l-Qasim Muhammad al-Mu‘tamid, Emir of Seville = I‘tamid </p>
<p>Zaida (Isabella) (1075-1107) (f3) = King Alfonso VI de León y Castile </p>
<p>Sancha, (F4) Princess of Leon and Castile = Rodrigo Gonzales (“El Franco”)</p>
<p>Rodrigo Rodriguez de Lara (b. 1123) = Elvira Garcia de Azarga</p>
<p>Sancha Rodriguez de Lara (f5) = Gonsalo Ruis II Giron (d. 1234)</p>
<p>Aldonza Gonzales de Giron (f6) = Ramiro Fruelaz, Señor de Cifontes </p>
<p>Aldonza Ramirez Fruelaz (f7) = Arias Diaz de Valderabano de Asturias</p>
<p>Maria de Henestrosa (f8) = Fernan Gonzalez de Henestrosa</p>
<p>Juan Fernández de Henestrosa = Sancha Gonzalez Villegas </p>
<p>Lope Alvarez de Henestrosa = Elvira Arias de Cuadros </p>
<p>Teresa López de Córdoba (f9) = Gomez Suarez de Figueroa </p>
<p>Lorenzo Suárez de Figueroa López de Córdoba, Maestre de Santiago = Maria Catalina de Orozco Garcia</p>
<p>Catalina Suárez de Figueroa (f10) = Inigo Lopez de Mendoza (120) </p>
<p>Mencía de Mendoza y Figueroa (f11) = Pedro Fernandez de Velasco</p>
<p>Leonor de la Vega y Velasco (f12) = Juan Tellez Giron</p>
<p>Juan Téllez Girón, "El Santo" = Maria de la Cueva y Toledo </p>
<p>Magdalena Girón de la Cueva (f13) = Juan Manuel Manrique de Lara</p>
<p>Luisa Manrique de Lara, duquesa de Nájera (f14) = Bernardo de Cardenas</p>
<p>Ana María de Cárdenas y Manrique de Lara (f15) = Jorge de Lancaster lll</p>
<p>MARIA GUADALUPE DE LANCASTER (f16) = MANUEL PONCE DE LEON</p>
<p>JOAQUIN PONCE DE LEON = ANNA MARIA SPINOLA DE LA CERDA</p>
<p>ANTONIO PONCE DE LEON = MARIA LUISA DE BOURBON</p>
<p>PABLO PONCE DE LEON EXILED TO CUYO, PHILIPPINES = GERTRUDES de AGUILAR - PALAWAN (130)</p>
<p>VICENTE PONCE DE LEON = CONSUELO ARGUELLES</p>
<p>CONSOLACION PONCE DE LEON (f17) = GREGORIO AUSTRIA</p>
<p>PABLO PONCE DE LEON AUSTRIA = FRANCISCA MACMAC</p>
<p>MARCELA AUSTRIA = MARIANO ZAMBALES (f18)</p>
<p>ELEUTERIO AUSTRIA ZAMBALES = JACOBA CARRANZA </p>
<p>MATIA CARRANZA ZAMBALES (f19) = FAUSTINO USTARIS</p>
<p>AGUSTIN ZAMBALES USTARIS = CARIDAD MAGBANUA PAREDES</p>
<p>AZUCENA USTARES (f20) = SABAS ROBLES ACOSTA</p>
<p>DATU ANDREI USTARES ACOSTA = ELLEN LAUROS BARRAQUIAS</p>
<p>ZIELLZA BARRAQUIAS ACOSTA (f21)</p>
<p>RAJA ANDREI BARRAQUIAS ACOSTA (141)</p>
<p>RESEARCH NOTES:</p>
<p>King of Kashmir and Gandhara</p>
<p>SOURCE NOTES:</p>
<p>http://www.american-pictures.com/genealogy/persons/per04842.htm#0</p>
<p>VICEROY OF TAXILA; POSSIBLY SAME AS "JALAUKA", KING OF KASHMIR AND GANDHARA
<p>(WHO WAS FATHER OF SAMPRATI, RULER OF THE UNITED MAURYA EMPIRE, c. 225/4 BC- c,</p>
<p>216/5 BC)</p>

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
